title: Foundation
subtitle: Morning activation — done before 8am
coaching_note: This is the habit. Not a workout — a ritual. 20 minutes, every morning, before the day gets in the way. On rest days, on travel days, on tired days. The bar is low by design.

exercises.name: Skip Rope
exercises.form_cue: Light on your feet. Land on the balls of your feet, not flat. If no rope, do jumping jacks or high knees instead.
exercises.why: Gets the heart rate up and body temperature rising fast. No better way to shake off morning grogginess.

phases.name: Activation
phases.coaching_note: These aren't hard. They're setting you up for the day and protecting the hips and back.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.form_cue: Drive through heels. Squeeze glutes at top and hold 1 second.
exercises.why: Wakes up the posterior chain before you load it during the day. Non-negotiable for lower back health.

exercises.name: Clamshell (pilates band)
exercises.form_cue: Lying on side, band just above knees, hips stacked. Rotate top knee up like a clamshell. Don't let the hip roll back.
exercises.why: Gluteus medius activation — directly targets the muscle group around the left iliac crest injury. Also protects knees on runs.

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
exercises.form_cue: Lunge forward, drop back knee. Place same-side hand on floor. Reach opposite hand to sky, rotating torso. Hold 2 seconds at the top.
exercises.why: One movement that hits hip flexors, thoracic rotation, hamstrings, and glutes. Best single exercise for morning mobility.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.form_cue: Lower back pressed into floor. Extend opposite arm and leg slowly. Return slowly. Don't rush.
exercises.why: Deep core activation. Sets the spine for the day.

exercises.name: Calf Raise
exercises.form_cue: On the edge of a step or flat floor. Rise all the way up, lower all the way down. Don't bounce.
exercises.why: Calf and Achilles tendon conditioning. As run volume builds, this is injury prevention on every morning.

progression_notes: On days when you have a strength or run session later, keep this light — it's activation, not fatigue. On rest days, you can extend the stretching section instead of doing the skip rope.
